themes: model competition (GPT-5.5, Grok Build, Codex continuation)

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Concrete measurement: Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x tokens for text and up to 3x for images vs 4.6 at the same per-token price — the most rigorous cost analysis in this feed.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost is resolution-dependent, not universal — changes the practical pricing story for builders using small images.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Sharp counterfactual: OpenAI could have kept reasoners secret and denied DeepSeek the blueprint — relevant for thinking about competitive strategy in AI product launches.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build + Grok Computer expected to ship as a unified desktop app — the most concrete competitive product update in this batch.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: "If your roadmap is >30 days, you're ngmi" — blunt framing for AI-era product velocity that's getting real traction (87K views, 67 reposts).

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues every human-built system (finance, communication, time management) needs reinvention from first principles for agents — directly relevant to agent-native product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest open-source model router claims 70% cost reduction by routing requests to cheaper models — a real tool for anyone managing multi-model agent infrastructure costs.

worthClicking.author: @kloss_xyz
worthClicking.why: Concrete Codex demo: 6 products (iOS app, web app, investor deck, launch video, landing page, X automation) built in under 2 hours for free — tests the indie dev distribution thesis.

voices.why: Only person in this feed doing rigorous empirical testing on Opus 4.7 token costs with actual tooling — essential for anyone making API budget decisions.
Sharp, non-hype takes on agent-native strategy and product velocity that match the operator's interests without the breathless demo content.
Historically grounded AI strategy perspective; the reasoner counterfactual thread is the kind of original thinking absent elsewhere in this feed.

examples: aakashgupta posting science/sports/film trivia unrelated to AI (NASA, tennis longevity, Dragon Ball)
RoundtableSpace crypto shilling (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots, 'aping in' engagement posts)
DataChaz/JaynitMakwana low-effort repost farming and newsletter spam
